sanskrit:
सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः।
तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता॥
transliteration:
sarva-yoniṣu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ sambhavanti yāḥ |
tāsāṃ brahma mahad-yonir ahaṃ bīja-pradaḥ pitā ||
literal_meaning: In all wombs, O son-of-Kuntī, whatever forms come into being—of them the Great-Womb is Brahman; I the seed-giving Father.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes identity: every body, mood, or role we inhabit is a temporary form born of Nature (the ‘womb’), while the conscious spark (the ‘seed’) is a gift from the transcendent Self. Psychologically, this dissolves over-identification with any single state and invites compassionate curiosity toward all inner sub-personalities.
psychological_state: Shifting from egoic ownership (“I am this mood/role”) to meta-positions of Self-as-observer and Self-as-loving-parent toward all inner parts.
trigger_scenario: A client says, “I hate how jealous I get; it’s not who I want to be.” The therapist gently replies, “That jealous part is one child in the nursery of your psyche; you also hold the whole nursery.”
anchor_text: You are the conscious spark, not the passing form.
